title: alloc_subscription_address race: concurrent requests may share a Sapling address
description: ws.rs alloc_subscription_address(): next_diversifier() atomically returns current and advances to current+1. But find_address(start) may skip invalid diversifiers and land on actual_u128 > start+1. advance_diversifier_to(actual_u128+1) is called afterward. A concurrent request that called next_diversifier() between start+1 and actual_u128 would also call find_address() and land on the same actual address. Both requests produce the same Sapling address. The second create_invoice() is silently ignored (INSERT OR IGNORE on UNIQUE address), so that client receives a stale invoice_id pointing to an address that is now linked to a different user's invoice. Payment to that address activates the first user's subscription, not the second's. Fix: use a single serialized transaction that does find_address and advance atomically, or hold a mutex around the two-step sequence.
